Hds::Button.description: Primary button component with various styles and sizes

@text.notes: Text content moves from @text attribute to default slot

@icon.notes: Icon moves to named slot — must be substituted with the correct Carbon SVG, no string name mapping exists

@isIconOnly.notes: No Carbon equivalent — icon-only button is implied by placing only an SVG in slot:icon with no text content. Remove this attribute after verifying only a slot:icon is present.

Hds::Button.notes: High confidence - direct 1:1 mapping with complete attribute coverage

Hds::Link::Standalone.notes: High confidence - straightforward mapping with minor attribute transformations

Hds::Link::Inline.description: Inline link rendered within text content

@color.notes: cds-link has no color prop — primary style is the default. Remove this attribute.

@icon.notes: cds-link has no icon slot. Place a Carbon SVG directly as a child element next to the text content.

@iconPosition.notes: No icon-position prop on cds-link. Control icon order via DOM order (leading = SVG before text, trailing = SVG after text).

default.notes: Yielded text content passes through to the cds-link default slot unchanged

Hds::Link::Inline.notes: Medium-high confidence. Text-only usages (no @icon) are straightforward swaps at 0.92+. Any usage with @icon drops to ~0.70 and requires manual Carbon SVG placement. @color is simply removed — cds-link only has one style.

Hds::Icon.description: Icon component — renders a Flight icon by string name

@name.notes: HDS takes a string name (e.g. 'arrow-right'). cds-icon takes a JS property binding: .icon="${ArrowRight16}" imported from '@carbon/icons/es/<name>/<size>.js'. There is no automatic string-to-import mapping — the correct Carbon icon must be identified manually and imported at the top of the file.

@size.notes: HDS supports 16 and 24. cds-icon supports 16, 20, 24, 32. Values map directly.

@color.notes: cds-icon has no color attribute. Apply color via CSS (fill or currentColor) on the element or a parent. HDS named colors (e.g. 'action', 'critical') must be translated to the equivalent CSS custom property or class.

@isInline.notes: cds-icon is always inline. Remove this attribute.

@stretched.notes: No cds-icon equivalent. Control sizing via CSS width/height on the element.

Hds::Icon.notes: Medium confidence. The @name → .icon transform is always manual — requires identifying the correct Carbon icon import from '@carbon/icons'. Size and aria-label map directly. Color must move to CSS. Usage without @color and with a standard size (16 or 24) can be partially auto-scaffolded but the import line must always be written by hand.

Hds::Badge.description: Badge/tag component for labels and status indicators

@color.notes: Color maps to type attribute in Carbon

Hds::Badge.notes: Medium-high confidence - Badge maps to Tag with some semantic differences in color handling

@type.notes: @type is the Helios display VARIANT ('page', 'inline', 'compact') — not the severity. Choose the correct CWC tag based on this value: 'inline' → cds-inline-notification, 'page' → cds-actionable-notification. Do NOT map @type to kind.

@color.notes: @color is the Helios severity — this maps to kind on cds-inline-notification

@onDismiss.notes: Dismissal requires manual implementation with close button

default.notes: Content may need to move to subtitle attribute

Hds::Alert.notes: Medium confidence - dismissal pattern differs, requires manual review

Hds::AppFrame.description: Application layout frame — no single Carbon equivalent, replaced by composing UI Shell elements

@hasHeader.notes: Presence of cds-header in the DOM is the Carbon equivalent — no boolean gate needed

@hasSidebar.notes: Presence of cds-side-nav in the DOM is the Carbon equivalent — no boolean gate needed

Header.notes: Frame.Header slot content becomes the contents of <cds-header> placed at the root of the page

Sidebar.notes: Frame.Sidebar slot content becomes the contents of <cds-side-nav> placed at the root of the page

Main.notes: Frame.Main slot content moves into a plain <main> or <div> element — Carbon UI Shell positions it via CSS

Footer.notes: No Carbon footer shell component — use a plain <footer> element if needed

Modals.notes: No Carbon modals container — modals render at root level directly

Hds::AppFrame.notes: Low-medium confidence. HdsAppFrame is a layout orchestrator with named slots. Carbon has no equivalent wrapper — the UI Shell components (cds-header, cds-side-nav) are placed directly in the document and positioned via CSS. Remove HdsAppFrame entirely and place cds-header, cds-side-nav, and a <main> element as siblings at the root level.

@hasA11yRefocus.notes: Helios-specific a11y refocus — no Carbon equivalent, remove

@breakpoint.notes: Carbon UI Shell handles its own responsive breakpoints internally

logo.notes: <:logo> named block containing HdsAppHeaderHomeLink becomes <cds-header-name> inside cds-header

globalActions.notes: <:globalActions> content (org switcher etc.) moves into <cds-header-nav> or header action area

utilityActions.notes: <:utilityActions> content (search, help, user menus) moves into header action slots. Icon-only buttons map to cds-header-menu-button or custom icon buttons in the header.

Hds::AppHeader.notes: Medium confidence. HdsAppHeader named slots (:logo, :globalActions, :utilityActions) map to Carbon header child elements. The responsive mobile menu behavior is built into cds-header automatically via cds-header-menu-button — no manual toggle logic needed.

Hds::AppHeader::HomeLink.description: Application logo/home link in the header

@text.notes: @text becomes the text content of cds-header-name

@icon.notes: Helios icon name has no Carbon equivalent — remove or replace with an inline SVG before cds-header-name

Hds::AppHeader::HomeLink.notes: Medium confidence. cds-header-name accepts href and text content directly. The @icon (e.g. 'hashicorp') has no Carbon equivalent — Carbon header name supports an optional prefix attribute (e.g. prefix='IBM') and text label. Example: <cds-header-name href='#' prefix='IBM'>HashiCorp</cds-header-name>

@isResponsive.notes: cds-side-nav is responsive by default — no attribute needed

@isCollapsible.notes: @isCollapsible={{true}} maps to collapse-mode='rail' on cds-side-nav (rail mode allows icon-only collapsed state)

@isMinimized.notes: No direct equivalent — cds-side-nav manages its own collapsed state

default.notes: Children of HdsAppSideNav become children of <cds-side-nav-items> inside cds-side-nav

Hds::AppSideNav.notes: Medium confidence. HdsAppSideNav maps to cds-side-nav with collapse-mode='rail'. Children (HdsAppSideNavList instances) must be wrapped in <cds-side-nav-items>. Responsive collapse behavior is handled automatically by Carbon.

Hds::AppSideNavList.description: Side navigation list group with title and links

Title.notes: SNL.Title has no direct Carbon equivalent inside cds-side-nav-items — Carbon uses cds-side-nav-menu as a collapsible group with a title attribute instead

Link.notes: SNL.Link maps to <cds-side-nav-link href='...'>text</cds-side-nav-link>. @isActive maps to the active attribute. @icon has no Carbon equivalent — remove.

Hds::AppSideNavList.notes: Medium confidence. HdsAppSideNavList wraps multiple SNL.Link items and an optional SNL.Title. In Carbon, a flat list of cds-side-nav-link elements goes inside cds-side-nav-items directly. Grouped sections with a title use cds-side-nav-menu with a title attribute wrapping cds-side-nav-menu-item elements. @badge on SNL.Link has no Carbon equivalent.

Hds::Dropdown.description: Dropdown menu component with toggle trigger and contextual menu items

@enableCollisionDetection.notes: Helios collision detection maps roughly to Carbon autoalign experimental property

ToggleButton.notes: dd.ToggleButton (labeled button trigger) → use cds-menu-button instead of cds-overflow-menu. Set label attribute to @text value.

ToggleIcon.notes: dd.ToggleIcon (icon-only trigger) → use cds-overflow-menu with SVG in slot='icon'. No direct @icon name mapping — must use Carbon SVG icon.

Interactive.notes: dd.Interactive @href links → cds-overflow-menu-item with href attribute

Separator.notes: dd.Separator → add divider attribute to the following cds-overflow-menu-item

Title.notes: dd.Title has no Carbon equivalent — remove or replace with a disabled cds-overflow-menu-item as a visual label

Description.notes: dd.Description has no Carbon equivalent — remove or incorporate into surrounding layout

Checkmark.notes: dd.Checkmark (selectable item) has no direct Carbon equivalent — requires custom implementation

Hds::Dropdown.notes: Low-medium confidence. HdsDropdown is richer than any single Carbon component. Icon-trigger dropdowns map to cds-overflow-menu; labeled-button triggers map to cds-menu-button. dd.Title, dd.Description, dd.Checkmark have no Carbon equivalents. Each dropdown instance must be evaluated individually. Requires manual migration.

Hds::Form::TextInput::Field.description: Text input field with label, helper text, and error message

@isInvalid.notes: Direct @isInvalid attr swap. If the invalid state is driven by a conditional block ({{#if}}<Field.Error>) rather than a @isInvalid attr, set invalid={{condition}} directly on cds-text-input instead — see Error slot notes.

Error.notes: <Field.Error>text</Field.Error> becomes invalid-text="text" attribute. The {{#if}} condition controlling it becomes the invalid boolean attribute on cds-text-input

Hds::Form::TextInput::Field.notes: Medium-high confidence. HdsFormTextInputField is a compound component (input + label + helper + error) — cds-text-input does the same job with flat attributes instead of contextual blocks. Field.Label → label attr, Field.HelperText → helper-text attr, Field.Error → invalid-text attr with invalid boolean. Component becomes self-closing with no children.

Hds::PageHeader.description: Page-level title and description header — no Carbon equivalent
Hds::PageHeader.notes:
No Carbon equivalent. HdsPageHeader is a semantic
wrapper with title/description/subtitle/actions slots. Replace with native HTML:
containing

for PH.Title and

for PH.Description. Any unused slots (Subtitle, Badges, Actions, IconTile, Breadcrumb) can be dropped if not in use.

Hds::ButtonSet.description: Button grouping layout wrapper — no Carbon equivalent needed
Hds::ButtonSet.notes: No Carbon equivalent. HdsButtonSet is a plain <div class='hds-button-set'> with flex layout. Replace with a plain <div> and apply display:flex + gap styling manually, or remove the wrapper entirely if buttons are inside a container that already handles spacing (e.g. cds-modal-footer uses cds-modal-footer-button directly with no wrapper needed).

Hds::Form.description: Form layout component with sections, header, separator and footer

@tag.notes: cds-form always renders as a form element — @tag arg is not needed

@sectionMaxWidth.notes: No equivalent in cds-form — apply max-width via CSS on the form or a wrapper div

Section.notes: F.Section is a plain <div class='hds-form__section'> — replace with a plain <div> and remove the F. prefix

Footer.notes: F.Footer is a plain <div class='hds-form__footer'> — replace with a plain <div> containing the buttons directly

Hds::Form.notes: Medium-high confidence. The outer HdsForm → cds-form swap is direct. F.Section and F.Footer contextual components are just layout divs with no Carbon equivalent — flatten them to plain <div> elements. cds-form-group is NOT the equivalent here — that is for grouping related fields like radio buttons under a fieldset.

Hds::Accordion.description: Accordion component for collapsible content sections

Item.notes: A.Item contextual component becomes <cds-accordion-item>. @isOpen={{true}} becomes the open boolean attribute.

toggle.notes: <:toggle>text</:toggle> named block becomes title="text" attribute on cds-accordion-item

content.notes: <:content>...</:content> named block becomes direct children of cds-accordion-item

Hds::Accordion.notes: Medium-high confidence - same concept, clean transforms. Named blocks (:toggle, :content) restructure to title attribute + direct children. Contextual A.Item becomes cds-accordion-item tag directly.

@onClose.notes: Helios uses an @onClose arg; Carbon fires a cds-modal-closed DOM event — wire via {{on 'cds-modal-closed' this.closeModal}} instead

Footer.notes: M.Footer yields MF.close — replace {{on 'click' MF.close}} with data-modal-close attribute on the button instead

openPattern.notes: Remove the {{#if}} block — use the open attribute directly on cds-modal instead

Hds::Modal.notes: Medium confidence - same concept but structural mismatch. Contextual slots (M.Header/Body/Footer) become explicit child elements. open/close pattern differs: Helios uses {{#if}} + @onClose arg; Carbon uses open attribute + cds-modal-closed event + data-modal-close on buttons.

Hds::Text.description: Text component - no direct Carbon equivalent
Hds::Text.notes: No direct Carbon equivalent - requires manual migration to native HTML elements or Carbon typography utilities
